mainMenuButton.name: System Options
mainMenuButton.hint:
{System Options}

Allows you to view and change the current game options.

system.name: General
system.hint:
{General Options}

Settings for the game's system functions as a program, as well as settings for the city's windows, main menu, and anything that is not included in the other categories.

videoSubtitles.name: Video Subtitles
videoSubtitles.hint:
{Video Subtitles}

This option will display subtitles on any of the animated cutscenes.

buildingOutlines.name: Town Building Outlines
buildingOutlines.hint:
{Town Building Outlines}

In the town screen, any accessible building will display an outline when the mouse cursor is placed over it.

spellBookAnimation.name: Spell Book Animation
spellBookAnimation.hint:
{Spell Book Animation}

Shows page turning animation in the spell book.

alternativeButtonClick.name: Alternative Click Sounds
alternativeButtonClick.hint:
{Alternative Click Sounds}

Separates the sound of pressing and releasing the button.

backgroundSound.name: Background Sounds
backgroundSound.hint:
{Background Sounds}

Allows background sounds of objects to play.

map.name: Adventure Map
map.hint:
{Adventure Map Options}

Settings for functions that are located on the adventure map or can be called from the adventure map.

autoSave.name: Enable Autosaves
autoSave.hint:
{Autosaves}

At the end of each turn, a save with the name "autosave.gm"  will be created within the week, and existing saves will be overwritten.

showRoute.name: Show Move Patch
showRoute.hint:
{Show Move Patch}

When checked, will display a hero's movement path without moving the hero.

moveReminder.name: Show Move Reminder
moveReminder.hint:
{Show Move Reminder}

When a player has any heroes with movement points remaining, they will asked to confirm an end turn order.

smoothMapScroll.name: Smooth Map scrolling
smoothMapScroll.hint:
Smooth Map Scrolling}

Allows you to smoothly scroll the Adventure Map by holding down the right mouse button.

quickCombat.name: Quick Combat Settings
quickCombat.hint:
{Quick Combat}

This option allows the computer to handle all combat calculations, and the player will only be shown the Combat Results screen.

quickCombatManual.name: Manual combat
quickCombatManual.hint: You will be in charge of your units in combat.

quickCombatMana.name: Quick combat with magic
quickCombatMana.hint: The computer includes your use of magic in its calculations.
quickCombatMana.menu: Use Quick Combat to resolve the battle

quickCombatManaFree.name: Quick combat without magic
quickCombatManaFree.hint: The computer does not include your use of magic in its calculations.
quickCombatManaFree.menu: Use Quick Combat but don't cast hero spells (conserve spell points)

quickCombatAsk.name: Always ask before combat
quickCombatAsk.hint: Before the battle, you will see a menu with these options.

playerSpeed.name: Your Heroes Speed
hints: Set hero speed to jump (no animation - fastest)

enemySpeed.name: Enemy Speed
hints: Set hero speed to jump (no animation - fastest)
Do not show movement other-color heroes

scrollSpeed.name: Map Scroll Speed
hints: Set map scroll speed to slow
Set map scroll speed to medium
Set map scroll speed to fast

combat.name: Combat
combat.hint:
{Combat Options}

Settings for functions that are located in the combat window or can be called from the combat window.

autoCombat.name: Auto-Combat Options
autoCombat.hint:
{Auto-Combat}

It allows you to customize what the computer can control during an auto-combat.

creatureInfo.name: Creature Info
creatureInfo.hint:
{Creature Info}

When you right-click on a unit, you will see various statistics about it.

showHexGrid.name: View Hex Grid

movementShadow.name: Movement Shadow

cursorShadow.name: Cursor Shadow

autoCreatures.name: Use Creatures
autoCreatures.hint: Gives control of your units to auto combat.

autoSpells.name: Use Spells
autoSpells.hint: During auto-combat your hero will cast spells.

autoCatapult.name: Use Catapult
autoCatapult.hint:
When laying siege to an enemy's castle, your catapult troops will decide which part of the fortifications to target. If your Ballistics skill is high enough, you will be able to direct the catapult's fire yourself.

If your hero has the Ballistics skill, this option will give that control to the computer during auto-combat.

autoBallista.name: Use Ballista
autoBallista.hint:
During combat, your ballista troops will decide which enemy units to target. If your Artillery skill is high enough, you will be able to direct the ballista's fire yourself.

If your hero has the Artillery skill, this option will give that control to the computer during auto-combat.

autoFirstAidTent.name: Use First Aid Tent
autoFirstAidTent.hint:
During combat, your first aid troops will choose who needs to be healed.  If your First Aid skill is high enough, you will be able to direct the first aid tent yourself.

If your hero has the First Aid skill, this option will give that control to the computer during auto-combat.

autoQuick.name: Instant Auto-Combat
autoQuick.hint:
{Instant Auto-Combat}

Auto-combat animations will not be played.

allStatistics.name: All Statistics

onlySpells.name: Spells Only

healthBar.name: Show Health Bars
healthBar.hint:
{Units Health Bars}

When the option is enabled, the unit's health will be displayed above the unit's health number in the selected position and color.

quit.hint: {Quit to Deskop}Quit Heroes of Might and Magic III.(hotkey on adventure map - Alt+F4; in the options window - Q)

text.color_health: Set colour for current stack health
text.color_loss: Set colour for stack health points lost
text.enable: Enable stack health points additional info
text.held: Activate only if hotkey is held
